tasks.title: Split require_repoprompt config into plan_required and tool_injection flags with iteration-aware behavior
evidence: crates/ralph/src/contracts.rs:108: require_repoprompt is a single Option<bool> field in AgentConfig.
docs/configuration.md:28: require_repoprompt is documented as a single boolean flag.
crates/ralph/src/run_cmd.rs:286-334: Multiple iterations are handled in a loop, with is_followup = iteration_index > 1.
crates/ralph/src/promptflow.rs:92-112: build_phase1_prompt receives policy.require_repoprompt and passes it to render_worker_phase1_prompt.
User request: Separate into 'repoprompt_plan_required' (injects context_builder planning instructions) and 'repoprompt_tool_injection' (only injects tooling reminders).

User request: When multiple iterations are enabled, do NOT honor 'repoprompt_plan_required' config for phase 1 of subsequent iterations (only include it in the first iteration). DO honor 'repoprompt_tool_injection' config for all iterations.
plan: Add two new fields to AgentConfig in contracts.rs: repoprompt_plan_required (Option<bool>) and repoprompt_tool_injection (Option<bool>).
Update AgentConfig::merge_from to merge the new fields.
Update Config::default() in contracts.rs to set appropriate defaults for both new fields.
Update the schema (schemas/config.schema.json) to include the two new fields.
Update PromptPolicy in promptflow.rs to have separate fields: repoprompt_plan_required and repoprompt_tool_injection.
Update run_cmd.rs to create PromptPolicy with both flags, checking iteration context (is_followup) when building phase 1 prompts.

Modify build_phase1_prompt in promptflow.rs to accept iteration context (is_followup) and conditionally exclude repoprompt_plan_required for followup iterations while always including repoprompt_tool_injection.
Update execute_phase1_planning in phases.rs to pass is_followup context to build_phase1_prompt.

Find all other usages of require_repoprompt in promptflow.rs and run_cmd/phases.rs and update logic to use the new flags appropriately (repoprompt_tool_injection for all phases, repoprompt_plan_required only for first iteration phase 1).
Add tests covering: both flags true, only plan_required true, only tool_injection true, both false, and iteration-specific behavior (plan_required excluded in followup iterations).
Update docs/configuration.md to document the new separate flags and iteration-specific behavior.
Deprecate but keep require_repoprompt for backward compatibility, mapping it to repoprompt_plan_required for migration.
tasks.request: Separate the repoprompt config settings into 2 configuration settings instead of 1. One config setting for 'repoprompt_plan_required' and one for 'repoprompt_tool_injection'. The first config injects the context_builder planning instructions while the latter only injects the repo prompt tooling reminders. When multiple iterations are enabled, do NOT honor the 'repoprompt_plan_required' config for phase 1 of the subsequent iterations. Only include it in the first iteration. DO honor the 'repoprompt_tool_injection' config for all iterations. Phase 1 of the subsequent iterations will still plan, just don't include the mandatory instructions to use the repo prompt context_builder.

tasks.title: Implement per-task output directory isolation
evidence: Runners share the same working directory; simultaneous or overlapping runs could interfere

tasks.title: Add concurrent task execution support with worker pools

tasks.request: Add a capability to do multiple iterations per task. You may ask, why? The reason is that some models are more capable than others. Oftentimes, a dumber model does syntactically correct and functional code, but it does it in a way that is sloppy. So by having that same dumber model run across the same task multiple times, it enables iteration that enhances outcomes. On the initial loop, the agents have to examine the current state, and then come up with the solution and implement the solution for the task at hand. This leads to a lot of context being used on exploration and contemplation. Context gets bloated, and quality decreases because a lot of irrelevant information ends up in the context. On the subsequent passes for the same task, since the task has already been achieved, it has more of a clear context to be able to focus on how to do it better instead of having to come up with the new implementation solution altogether. Iteration enhances outcomes and leads to better outcomes. So for example, keep the three-phased approach, but do two loops on the same task, on the second go-round, it will be prompted that it had already been executed once before, and on the second go-round, it makes sure to identify any unexpected or unplanned behavior changes, poor coding habits. Outstanding follow-ups that need to be executed. And just does overall refinement to make it a better approach. Think of it like multiple senior developers doing like a peer review refinement process per task. This will lead to higher outcomes. Currently, the three-phase approach consists of phase one planning, phase two implementing the plan, and phase three completing any outstanding work that remained after phase two, as well as doing a thorough review for bugs, regressions, etc. Phase three is essentially like a code review, but it can also make changes. We will likely want to tweak the prompts for the second loop, or what. I want to avoid using any verbiage in the second and any subsequent loops that denotes that it is doing a second pass or a third pass, etc., because that may cause the agent to get a little lazy and assume that things are already done. I want to encourage the comprehensive approach liDon't repeat yourself as certainly a principle I want in place. Code readability, code maintainability, concise code, elegantly simple code but not feature or functionally simple code. I think it would be beneficial to be able to have different levels of reasoning applied for the initial pass of the task and the subsequent passes. I could see some potential downsides, but the reason to do this is to fold one to decrease runtime and for cost purposes, but if we have a model run on the first pass with high reasoning, it can spend a lot of time on doing it correctly or mostly correct on that initial pass and then on subsequent runs, since the task is overall a bit easier due to existing solutions being in place, we could have a lower reasoning level configured. We already allow for the reasoning effort to be controlled globally and by project in the config file, as well as have task-specific reasoning set in the task queue.

tasks.title: Investigate phase3 log risks and close gaps
evidence: User request: investigate Phase 3 review log items the agent flagged as suspicious and did not act on, and correct any issues found.
User-provided Phase 3 log highlights potential gaps in placeholder validation, iteration/phase behavior, CI gate enforcement, repo cleanliness checks, and config/docs consistency.
plan: Extract the suspicious items from the provided Phase 3 log into a checklist mapped to the affected modules, prompts, tests, and docs.
Reproduce or validate each item with targeted tests or minimal scenarios to confirm whether it is a bug, expected behavior, or a doc/schema mismatch.
Implement fixes or clarifying documentation for confirmed issues, preferring centralized helpers when the same pattern appears elsewhere.
Add or update tests to cover each resolved issue and its failure modes (no untested changes).
Update schemas/docs/examples affected by the fixes and run `make ci` to validate.
notes: Suspicious items called out in the Phase 3 log: placeholder-like patterns causing unresolved-placeholder false positives; raw vs escaped iteration context mismatch in render/validation; missing or inconsistent support for ITERATION_COMPLETION_BLOCK in phase2 handoff; preview prompt builder omits iteration context/completion blocks; phase3 non-final iterations skip CI gate enforcement; follow-up iterations may not refresh cached phase2 outputs; allow_dirty_repo baselining allows edits to already-dirty files; follow-up reasoning effort ignored for Codex without warning; iteration default mismatch between schema/docs; completion signal files lingering in 2-phase runs; lack of backoff/scheduling for follow-up iterations; potential config boolean expansion quirks in prompt rendering.
enforced baseline dirty-path invariants in Phase 1 plan-only runs
added tests for baseline dirty path enforcement and updated plan-only continue test

tasks.title: Add per-task model_effort override for runner reasoning effort
evidence: User request: Add support for a 'model_effort' field in the queue schema with default 'default' that overrides config reasoning_effort per task; support low/medium/high/xhigh and align accepted levels accordingly.
plan: Update the queue task schema and parsing/validation to accept optional model_effort with default handling and supported values (default, low, medium, high, xhigh).
Wire model_effort through runner selection so it overrides config reasoning_effort when the runner/model supports it (codex), and falls back to config when default or absent.
Align reasoning effort enum/validation across config and runners to the codex-supported levels and document any breaking changes.
Add tests for schema validation, config fallback, and runner override behavior for supported/unsupported values.
Update docs (queue-and-tasks, configuration, CLI help if needed) to describe model_effort behavior and examples.
notes: Add per-task model_effort override with default handling for Codex.
Align reasoning effort levels to low/medium/high/xhigh and update parsing.
Update schemas/docs/tests for queue/config/task behavior and overrides.
tasks.request: Add support for a 'model_effort' field in the queue schema. This field defaults to a value of 'default'. If the runner and model executing the task (currently only codex runner) supports a reasoning effort level the value of 'model_effort' overrides the global and repo config value of reasoning_effort when executing this task. A value of 'default', or if 'model_effort' is excluded, uses the value provided by the config. This enables users to specify variable reasoning efforts based on the task. The task builder should prefer to lean on 'default' but it may choose to set the value to any of the following (all of which are supported by codex, the only runner supporting this at the moment): low, medium (codex default), high, xhigh. If our accepted reasoning levels in ralph differ from these then update them accordingly to match.

tasks.title: Add 'update-task' option for pre-run priming
evidence: User request: Add a config option and flag for the 'run one' and 'run loop' commands to invoke 'ralph task update <TASK-ID>' before the supervisor run/loop begins the phases for the run.
Verified `ralph task update` is implemented in `crates/ralph/src/commands/task.rs` as `update_task_impl`.
Verified `RunAgentArgs` in `crates/ralph/src/agent.rs` is flattened into both `RunOneArgs` and `RunLoopArgs` in `crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs`.
plan: Add `update_task_before_run: Option<bool>` to `AgentConfig` in `crates/ralph/src/contracts.rs`.
Update `merge_from` and `Default` implementations in `contracts.rs` to handle the new field.
Add `--update-task` flag to `RunAgentArgs` in `crates/ralph/src/agent.rs`.
In `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs`, modify `run_one_impl` to execute `update_task_impl` (from `crate::commands::task`) before `mark_task_doing` if enabled.
Ensure the update step only runs once per Task-ID (handled by `run_one_impl` logic if called before the iteration loop).
Run `make generate` to update `schemas/config.schema.json`.
Update `docs/configuration.md` and `docs/cli.md` to document the new field and flag.
Verify implementation with `make ci` and manual testing.
notes: The update should happen before `mark_task_doing` to ensure the agent receives the refreshed task state in its first phase prompt.
We should use the same runner/model settings for the update as specified for the run itself.
tasks.request: Add a config option and flag for the 'run one' and 'run loop' commands to invoke 'ralph task update <TASK-ID>' before the supervisor run/loop begins the phases for the run. By updating the task before the phase 1 agent, or single phase agent, begins, we will be priming the agents with better task information. This new toggle and step ONLY executes if enabled and only runs ONCE per Task-ID, before the Phase or Phases begin. Meaning, during runs with iterations=2 or more this executes BEFORE the first iteration and does NOT run before the subsequent iterations. This is NOT an added "Phase" it is an optional step (disabled by default) that can be enabled per run via flag or in the config.json file. Make sure docs reflect the usage and configuration. Example that I do currently. Say task-ID RQ-0001 was next in the queue. I run 'ralph task update RQ-0001' and let it finish, then I run 'ralph run loop --max-tasks 1' and it executes the task. After this change if I enable the config I can just run 'ralph run loop --max-tasks 1' and it will update the task before running the task.

tasks.request: Add support for a new runner named 'cursor'. This runner should have feature parity with the other runners, such as opencode. The default 'model' for this runner is 'auto'. Custom values are supported. User is responsible for configuring a supported model name. The user should be told to run 'agent models' to determine which models are supported for their account. The cursor runner uses the binary 'agent'. See 'agent --help' for more information. I have provided some examples of how to use this agent below. Planning phase: 'agent --model auto --sandbox enabled --plan --print --output-format stream-json "Create a plan for the task ..."' See the output of this command (e.g. for JSON parsing) in the file at '/tmp/cursor-agent-plan.md'. It contains 'session_id' which is needed for the follow up messages when applicable. For non-planning phases, use the following command for reference: 'agent --model auto --sandbox disabled --print --output-format stream-json "List the files in this repo, then get the file tree via Repo Prompt, and then finish by creating a file in /tmp/cursor-agent-test.txt that simply contains the text Hello World.". The output of this command was written to the file '/tmp/cursor-agent-test.txt'. To resume a session with the agent use the following command syntax: 'agent --model auto --sandbox disabled --print --output-format stream-json --resume 64e5dea1-0cc6-4f98-b07b-fbc150fdad78 "Try RP again"'.

tasks.title: Fix persisting lock directory for 'task' commands
evidence: User reported lock file persisting after 'ralph task' and not cleaning up.
fsutil.rs: acquire_dir_lock sets remove_dir_on_drop: false for 'task' label.
DirLock::drop only attempts to remove lock_dir if remove_dir_on_drop is true.
When 'ralph task' runs, it creates the lock dir and a sidecar owner file. Upon exit, it removes the sidecar but leaves the empty lock dir.
Subsequent runs fail because acquire_dir_lock sees an existing lock dir but no 'owner' file (read_lock_owner returns None), triggering a 'Queue lock already held' error.
plan: Modify DirLock::drop in crates/ralph/src/fsutil.rs to attempt fs::remove_dir(&self.lock_dir) regardless of remove_dir_on_drop flag.
Ensure the removal attempt suppresses errors (it will fail harmlessly if directory is not empty/still in use).
This ensures that the last process exiting a shared lock directory cleans it up.
Add a test case in crates/ralph/tests/fsutil_test.rs to verify sequential task execution (acquire 'task' lock -> drop -> acquire again) succeeds.
Run `make ci` to verify the fix and ensure no regressions.
notes: This change ensures that 'ralph task' (which uses shared locking) cleans up the lock directory when it's the last process to exit, preventing subsequent runs from seeing a stale empty directory.
Modified DirLock::drop to always attempt best-effort removal of lock directory
Updated acquire_dir_lock to clean up if owner file write fails
Removed remove_dir_on_drop field from DirLock
Added regression test task_lock_cleans_up_directory_when_no_supervisor
Verified with make ci

tasks.title: Standardize timestamp handling
evidence: timeutil.rs only provides now_utc_rfc3339 helpers with a fixed 9-digit format; it does not expose shared parse/format helpers.
reports.rs defines a local parse_ts helper that calls OffsetDateTime::parse with Rfc3339.

queue/prune.rs parses completed_at via OffsetDateTime::parse while separately using timeutil for the current timestamp.
queue/repair.rs, queue/operations/edit.rs, and queue/operations/validate.rs each parse RFC3339 timestamps directly via OffsetDateTime::parse.
plan: Add timeutil::parse_rfc3339(ts: &str) -> Result<OffsetDateTime> and timeutil::format_rfc3339(dt: OffsetDateTime) -> Result<String> so parsing/formatting share the fixed 9-digit format.

Add/adjust tests to cover valid/invalid RFC3339 parsing and ensure 9-digit subsecond formatting is enforced.
Run `make ci` to validate queue validation, reports, and repair paths.
notes: reports.rs still owns a parse_ts helper; migrate it to timeutil for shared parsing behavior.
prune.rs already uses timeutil for now timestamps but parses completed_at locally, which is another consolidation target.

tasks.title: Achieve feature parity across all runners

Streaming output parsing only attempts full-line JSON and falls back to emitting raw lines, so non-JSON interleaving is handled as passthrough today (stream.rs).

`crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`: input loop only matches `Event::Key`, so mouse events are ignored.
`crates/ralph/src/tui/events/mod.rs`: `handle_key_event` only accepts `KeyEvent`; no mouse handler exists.
`crates/ralph/src/tui/render/panels.rs`: `draw_task_list` only records `app.list_height`, so list panel bounds aren't available for click hit-testing.
plan: Store the task list panel bounds on `App` during render so mouse clicks can be mapped to list rows.
Handle `Event::Mouse` in the main input loop and translate wheel up/down into list or details scrolling based on the focused panel.
On left-click inside the list bounds, update focus and selection and clamp scroll via existing helpers.
Add tests for wheel scrolling and click selection (including clicks outside the list and empty lists) in `crates/ralph/src/tui/events/tests.rs`.
Run `make ci`.
notes: Mouse hit-testing needs list bounds; only `list_height` is stored today.
Added mouse scroll-up coverage for list/details.
Mouse wheel and click selection behavior validated by tests.
Updated RQ-0330 stale note about mouse events.
CI: make ci

tasks.title: Fix Phase 3 completion race: ensure terminal archive + completion artifact despite dirty repo
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/phase3.rs`: `ensure_phase3_completion` validates queue+done and errors unless the task is archived in done.json with status Done/Rejected.

`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/phase3.rs`: when git commit/push is enabled, Phase 3 requires a clean repo, ignoring only `.ralph/config.json` for Done and using `RALPH_RUN_CLEAN_ALLOWED_PATHS` for Rejected.
`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/phase3.rs`: `apply_phase3_completion_signal` reads `.ralph/cache/completions/<task>.json`, updates queue/done, then removes the signal file.
`crates/ralph/src/cli/task.rs`: under supervision, `ralph task done` writes a completion signal instead of directly updating queue/done.
.ralph/cache/completions exists and currently contains RQ-0318.json.
plan: Review Phase 3 completion loop (apply completion signal, `post_run_supervise`, `ensure_phase3_completion`) and confirm the ordering of archive checks vs git-clean enforcement.
Reproduce the failure path by simulating a supervised completion signal plus dirty repo (queue/done updated) to see when the archive check or clean-repo check trips.
Adjust ordering/logic so the terminal archive status is checked after queue/done updates and completion signals are persisted, without treating queue/done edits as an incomplete Phase 3.
If clean-repo enforcement needs to tolerate queue/done updates, update the allowlist or add a targeted exception with clear justification.
Add tests for success and failure modes: completion signal present/absent, task archived vs missing, and dirty repo with queue/done updates.
Update CLI docs/help if the Phase 3 completion requirements or `ralph task done` behavior changes.
Run `make ci`.
notes: Completion signal artifacts are stored under `.ralph/cache/completions` and the directory is currently populated (RQ-0318.json), so the completion-signal path is active in this repo.
Finalize Phase 3 when completion signal is missing but task is already archived.
Make completion signals idempotent and add tests for archived/no-signal completion.
Centralize unit-test git helpers and update tests to use them.

tasks.title: Scan: show validation error details before prompting for post-run action
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/commands/scan.rs`: post-run validation failure calls `apply_git_revert_mode` before building the `context`, so the revert prompt runs before the error text is attached.
`crates/ralph/src/runutil.rs`: `prompt_revert_choice` prints the action prompt to stderr immediately, explaining why the prompt appears before error details.
`crates/ralph/src/commands/scan.rs`: the safeguard stdout path is appended in `context` ("Scan validation failed after run." + `safeguard_msg`) only after the prompt is handled.
plan: Inspect scan post-run validation and revert prompting to confirm current output order.
Adjust the post-run validation error path to surface validation details (including safeguard path) before prompting for the post-run action.
Ensure revert prompt handlers (CLI/TUI) still behave correctly after the reorder.
Add/extend tests (near scan or in `crates/ralph/src/runutil_tests.rs`) asserting error details are emitted before the prompt for keep/revert paths.
Update `docs/cli.md` if the user-visible flow or example output changes.
Run `make ci`.
notes: Current post-run failure path in `scan.rs` defers message formatting until after prompting, so the prompt precedes the error output.
Show scan validation details before revert prompt via preface context.
TUI confirm-revert overlay renders preface above the action prompt.
Added prompt ordering tests and updated runutil_tests module docs.
make ci

tasks.title: Scan UX: expand `.repo_ignore` to exclude noisy generated/runtime paths
evidence: `.repo_ignore` currently contains only `!**`, `.DS_Store`, and `target/`, so scans and tooling may include `.ralph/cache` and other runtime artifacts.
`.gitignore` already ignores `.ralph/cache/*` and `.ralph/logs/`, suggesting these should also be excluded from scan/tool context by default.
plan: Decide the intended meaning of `.repo_ignore` (tooling/scan ignore list) and document it briefly.
Add ignore entries for common generated/runtime paths (e.g., `.ralph/cache/`, `.ralph/logs/`, `.ralph/lock`, `target/`).
Ensure the ignore rules do not exclude source-of-truth files like `.ralph/queue.json`.
Run `make ci`.

tasks.title: Public defaults: make destructive actions opt-in (git push/commit, YOLO permissions)
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/contracts/config.rs`: `Config::default()` sets `agent.git_commit_push_enabled: Some(true)` and `agent.claude_permission_mode: Some(ClaudePermissionMode::BypassPermissions)`, and runner CLI defaults include `approval_mode: Some(RunnerApprovalMode::Yolo)`.
`schemas/config.schema.json`: schema defaults include `agent.git_commit_push_enabled: true` and `claude_permission_mode: bypass_permissions`.
`crates/ralph/src/commands/init.rs`: `write_config` writes only `{ "version": 1 }`, so new installs inherit built-in defaults.
`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision.rs`: `post_run_supervise` calls `finalize_git_state(...)` and performs pushes when `git_commit_push_enabled` is true.
plan: Define safe-by-default behavior for public users (disable auto git commit/push; avoid bypassing approvals by default).
Update `Config::default()` and schema defaults (`config schema`) to match the new safety posture.
Update `ralph init` to write an explicit starter `.ralph/config.json` that encodes the safe defaults (not just `{"version":1}`).
Update docs (`README.md`, `docs/configuration.md`) to explain the defaults and how to opt into auto-commit/push and relaxed approval modes.
Update/extend tests that assert default config behavior and supervisor git behavior.
Run `make ci`.

tasks.title: Scan/Task builder: stop forcing bypass permissions; respect config/flags
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/commands/scan.rs`: `run_scan` sets `permission_mode = Some(ClaudePermissionMode::BypassPermissions)` with an explicit “Force BypassPermissions for scan” comment.
`crates/ralph/src/commands/task.rs`: task builder and task updater both set `permission_mode = Some(ClaudePermissionMode::BypassPermissions)` (forced bypass in multiple code paths).
`crates/ralph/src/agent.rs`: `AgentArgs` used by scan/task exposes runner/model/effort/repo-prompt only—no approval or permission overrides are available for those commands.
`crates/ralph/src/contracts/config.rs`: config supports `agent.claude_permission_mode` plus `runner_cli.defaults.approval_mode`, but scan/task ignore them today.
plan: Define the desired precedence for scan/task/task-update permission behavior (explicit CLI flags > config runner_cli/claude_permission_mode > runner defaults) and document the per-runner mapping.
Remove hardcoded `BypassPermissions` in scan, task builder, and task updater; plumb resolved permission/approval settings into the runner invocation.
If CLI overrides are required, extend `AgentArgs` + scan/task clap wiring and update `--help`/`docs/cli.md` (ensure CLI/TUI parity or document any divergence).
Add tests covering scan/task builder/task updater for config defaults, CLI overrides, and non-interactive failure modes when approvals would block.
Run `make ci`.
notes: Task updater (`ralph task update`) currently forces bypass permissions the same way as the task builder; treat it as part of the same fix.
Run commands already expose approval/sandbox CLI overrides via `RunAgentArgs`; scan/task do not, so reuse that pattern or document why not.
Removed forced Claude bypass for scan/task/task update; now use config + runner_cli resolution.
Added shared RunnerCliArgs parsing for scan/task and plumbed runner CLI overrides through options.
Updated CLI/docs/help examples and tests for approval/sandbox overrides and permission precedence.
make ci

tasks.title: Security: redact or gate safeguard output dumps (avoid writing secrets to disk)
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/fsutil.rs:129-138`: `safeguard_text_dump` writes raw `content` to `output.txt` without redaction; no opt-in mechanism exists.
`crates/ralph/src/commands/scan.rs:182,222`: calls `fsutil::safeguard_text_dump` with raw `output.stdout` on queue read/validation failures.
`crates/ralph/src/debuglog.rs`: module logs raw runner output when `--debug` is enabled; this is intentional but should be documented as a security consideration.
`crates/ralph/src/redaction.rs:128-139`: `redact_text()` function exists and masks API keys, bearer tokens, AWS keys, SSH keys, hex tokens, and sensitive env values.
plan: Add `safeguard_text_dump_redacted` function in `fsutil.rs` that applies `redaction::redact_text` before writing.
Modify existing `safeguard_text_dump` to require explicit opt-in (env var `RALPH_RAW_DUMP=1` or `--debug` flag) with loud warnings.
Update `scan.rs` error paths to use redacted dumps by default; only use raw dumps when opt-in is enabled.
Add unit tests in `fsutil.rs` proving secrets are redacted in dumps unless opt-in is enabled.
Update `docs/cli.md` to document safeguard dump behavior and the `--debug` security implications.
Update `docs/workflow.md` to mention redaction behavior for debug logs and safeguard dumps.
Run `make ci`.
notes: Redaction module already exists and handles: API keys, bearer tokens, AWS keys, SSH keys, hex tokens (32+ chars), sensitive env vars.
Debug logging intentionally keeps raw output; this is acceptable with explicit opt-in (--debug) but needs clear documentation.
Consider adding a config option `agent.redact_dumps: true` (default) for fine-grained control.
Implemented safeguard dump redaction to prevent secrets from being written to disk
Added safeguard_text_dump_redacted() function for redacted dumps by default
Modified safeguard_text_dump() to require explicit opt-in (RALPH_RAW_DUMP=1 or --debug)
Updated scan.rs and runutil.rs to use redacted dumps by default
Added comprehensive unit tests for redaction behavior
Updated docs/cli.md and docs/workflow.md with security documentation

tasks.request: We need to consider migrations when we implement changes to the config, .ralph/README.md, etc. Users aren't updating .ralph/READEME.md, or at least shouldn't be as it is mainly there for agents, but they might, but when we change content of the READEME.md we need to ensure we are updating .ralph/READEME.md in other projects after they run ralph after updating. Same thing for checking config. If we change the name of a config key to a new value we should be migrating that value for the user and keeping what they set that config param to. If we decide to change the default task queue.json to queue.jsonc (which we just added support for) then we should consider migrating that safely too. These are things that users expect to be seamless.

tasks.request: I believe we added support for model_effort in the task queue. this is primarily only beneficial for the codex runner with GPT models but just wanted to make sure we have support for that and it is documented. If it is added during task creation and an unsupported runner/model is used it is just ignored (intended) but it doesn't hurt to have it added. Acceptable values are (low, medium, high, xhigh). xhigh should only be used very rarely as it consumes usage limits extremely fast. both task builder and scan can utilize this field as long as we clarify its purpose. the purpose is to dynamically override the model reasoning effort via this optional field so users can run loops without having to stop and change their config as varying levels of tasks are in progress.

tasks.request: Read the plan at this file .ralph/cache/plans/RQ-0454.md. Under key design decisions, number four, it says run automatically on Ralph's startup before command execution, and I agree with that. I want that. That is the intended behavior I want. Like, if someone runs Ralph, whether they run any command for the first time, like they open a command prompt in a project and they run Ralph. With the exception of Ralph in it to initialize. If they run Ralph run, for example, it should do the automatic check. If they run Ralph run one, it should do the automatic check. I want to make sure that it doesn't rerun that check again and again and again in the same session though. So it does it once at startup. But as the loop continues, it shouldn't be rechecking during the loop. And it shouldn't be checking during the queue validation. It should really only be run like the execution of the checks should really only be run when it's used with 'ralph run' 'ralph doctor' 'ralph task' 'ralph queue'. What I dont want to happen is I run 'ralph run loop --max-tasks 5' and see the validation snanity check happening several times. It should do the sanity check once initially and then not again until 'ralph run loop --max-tasks5' terminates and the user invokes another 'ralph' command. Otherwise it just adds meaningless overhead. Maybe this is how it is already implemented, or there's no reason for concern due to how the workflows and code works, but I wanted to call this out and have a task built to have this clarified, and fixed if it doesnt align with my expectations. If the current implementation is valid then no changes are required.

tasks.title: Cache prompt expansion regexes
evidence: expand_variables() in util.rs:223 constructs Regex::new(r"\$\$\{|\\\$\{").unwrap()
expand_variables() in util.rs:226 constructs Regex::new(r"\$\{([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)(:-([^}]*))?\}").unwrap()
expand_variables() in util.rs:247 constructs Regex::new(r"\{\{config\.([^}]+)\}\}").unwrap()
These three regexes are static patterns and should be cached with std::sync::LazyLock
Project uses Rust 1.93 which supports std::sync::LazyLock (stabilized in 1.80)

tasks.title: Fix race condition in shared lock mode for task labels
evidence: path: crates/ralph/src/lock.rs :: acquire_dir_lock assigns owner files for label "task" using only owner_task_<pid>, so concurrent task locks in the same process share and overwrite the same sidecar file

path: crates/ralph/src/lock.rs :: DirLock::drop always calls cleanup_lock_dir, which removes owner_path then attempts to remove the lock directory even when a supervising owner file or another task sidecar is still present
path: crates/ralph/tests/task_lock_coexistence_test.rs :: shared lock tests cover a single task lock with a supervisor but do not cover multiple concurrent task locks from one process
plan: Define a unique per-lock owner sidecar name for "task" locks (pid + unique suffix) and update acquire_dir_lock to use it without clobbering
Skip directory removal in cleanup when the owner file is a task sidecar and other owner files remain, to avoid interfering with supervising locks
Update doctor lock validation to recognize the updated owner_task naming scheme
Add tests for concurrent task locks in the same process and for cleanup with multiple task sidecars plus a supervising lock
Run targeted lock tests (e.g., cargo test -p ralph task_lock_coexistence_test lock_race_cleanup_test)
notes: Shared task lock behavior is already covered for single supervisor+task cases; concurrency with multiple task locks in one process is untested.

Added cleanup logic to prevent task locks from removing directory when other owners remain

tasks.title: Harden Ctrl+C handling in runner execution to prevent stuck processes
evidence: path: crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/process.rs :: run_with_streaming_json clears ctrlc.active_pgid only after wait_for_child succeeds; timeouts or IO errors return early and skip cleanup.
path: crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/process.rs :: ctrlc::set_handler result is ignored, so handler setup failures are silent.
path: crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/process.rs :: ctrlc.interrupted is reset at run start even if a Ctrl+C occurred before spawn.
path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs :: run_one_impl resets the global Ctrl+C flag before task execution.
path: crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/tests/process.rs :: tests cover wait_for_child timeout/Ctrl-C flows but not cleanup on error paths or pre-run interrupts.
plan: Add a cleanup guard in run_with_streaming_json to clear ctrlc.active_pgid and join stdout/stderr readers on all exit paths, including timeout/IO errors.
Define and implement policy for pre-run Ctrl+C (skip spawn or return Interrupted) and avoid clearing the flag when an interrupt is already set.

notes: SIGINT/SIGKILL grace handling already exists; remaining gaps are cleanup on error paths and visibility into handler setup failures.

tasks.title: Allow auto-resolve to proceed when git merge reports conflicts
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:179-182 routes `ConflictPolicy::AutoResolve` into `resolve_conflicts`, which is intended to handle dirty/conflicted PRs.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:226-233 runs `git merge origin/<base>` via the local `git_run` before calling `conflict_files`; `git merge` commonly exits with code 1 when conflicts are present.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:331-340 treats any non-success exit as fatal, so merge conflicts can abort before the conflict-detection/AI-resolution path runs.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:300-307 detects conflicts via `git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U`, which can only run if the merge step doesn't early-abort on conflict exit status.

crates/ralph/src/git/error.rs:91-123 provides `git_base_command` and a `git_run` that preserves `status.code()`, enabling a merge-specific helper to allow exit code 1 without changing other git command semantics.
plan: Add a merge-specific helper (e.g., `git_merge_allow_conflicts`) that runs `git merge` and treats exit code 1 as ‘conflicts present’ (continue), while erroring on other non-zero exit codes.
Use the helper in `resolve_conflicts` so `conflict_files`/AI resolution can proceed even when the merge reports conflicts.

Refactor the checkout+merge preparation into a testable function and add unit tests (using temp git repos + a remote) covering clean merge (no conflicts) and conflicted merge (exit 1) continuing to conflict detection.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph merge_runner`.
notes: `merge_runner.rs` currently duplicates git invocation helpers (`git_run`/`git_output`) instead of using `crate::git::error::git_run`; prefer a narrowly-scoped merge helper rather than changing all git command behavior.
Added git_merge_allow_conflicts helper in git/error.rs to treat merge exit code 1 as 'conflicts present' (continue)

tasks.title: Prune stale parallel tasks when stored PID is no longer running
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:137-154 prunes `tasks_in_flight` only when `workspace_path` is missing; it does not check `record.pid` liveness.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:270-272 stores the spawned worker PID (`child.id()`) into `ParallelTaskRecord`, but that PID is not used during stale-task pruning.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/state.rs:87-102 includes `pid: Option<u32>` in `ParallelTaskRecord` and persists it to `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json`.
crates/ralph/src/lock.rs:523-573 already implements cross-platform `pid_is_running(pid: u32) -> Option<bool>` that can be reused for parallel stale-task pruning.
plan: Extract stale-task pruning into a helper that drops records when the workspace is missing OR `pid_is_running(pid)` returns `Some(false)`; treat `None`/missing pid as indeterminate and retain the record.
Call the helper after loading the parallel state in `run_loop_parallel`, log any dropped task IDs, and persist the updated state file.
Add unit tests covering: missing workspace drops; dead PID with existing workspace drops; missing PID with existing workspace is retained.
Verify with `cargo test -p ralph parallel` (and run `make ci` before merging).
notes: `crate::lock::pid_is_running` already exists (Unix + Windows) and is the simplest way to avoid duplicating process-liveness logic for parallel state pruning.
Parallel state tasks use `workspace_path` (legacy `worktree_path` is explicitly rejected in `parallel/state.rs` tests), so any stale-record cleanup should focus on `workspace_path` + `pid`.
Extracted stale-task pruning into prune_stale_tasks_in_flight() helper
Drops records when workspace missing OR pid_is_running() returns Some(false)
Retains records when PID missing or status indeterminate (None)
Added 4 unit tests covering all edge cases

tasks.title: Ensure parallel loop cleans up workers on non-interrupt errors
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:232-329 uses `?` on task selection, workspace creation/state sync, worker spawn, and `child.try_wait`, so any of these errors returns early from `run_loop_parallel`.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:347-377 performs termination/workspace cleanup and clears `state_file.tasks_in_flight` only when `interrupted` is true (Ctrl+C path).

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:160-185 spawns the merge runner thread; on non-interrupt early returns there is no `merge_stop` signal or join, so the merge runner may continue detached after the parallel loop exits with an error.
plan: Refactor `run_loop_parallel` to use a Drop-based cleanup guard (patterned after `ProcessCleanupGuard` in `crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/process.rs`) that stops/joins merge runner, terminates in-flight workers, removes workspaces, and persists a cleared parallel state on any early return.
Disarm the guard only on the clean-success path after all workers and merge results have been fully drained and state has been persisted.

Add unit tests in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs` that spawn a long-lived child (e.g., `sleep 10`) in `in_flight`, then drop the guard to assert the process is killed and the state cleanup path runs; include a test that the merge runner thread observes `merge_stop` and is joined.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph parallel`.
notes: Line references updated on 2026-02-03; cleanup gating is currently only the Ctrl+C (`interrupted`) branch.
Because the merge runner thread uses `thread::spawn` and is only joined on success/interrupt, early error returns can leave a detached merge runner running against workspaces/state.
Fixed critical integration issue: workers and workspaces are now properly registered in the cleanup guard
Eliminated RefCell to prevent panic during unwinding - guard now uses &mut self methods
Moved mark_completed() to after all cleanup steps succeed on success path
Added Drop-path test to verify cleanup runs automatically when guard is dropped
Removed dead code: handle_worker_success/failure functions, collect_workspaces_for_cleanup
CI passes with 1679 tests

tasks.title: Prevent duplicate task selection across concurrent run loops
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs:46-66 acquires the queue lock, loads the queue, and returns (task_id, task_title) without reserving the task (no status update or queue write).

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:244-291 selects a task, creates a workspace, spawns the worker, and only persists in-flight tracking to `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json` (no reservation in `.ralph/queue.json`).

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs:452-459 only holds the queue lock for `QueueLockMode::Acquire`; `run_one_parallel_worker` uses `QueueLockMode::Skip` (crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs:359-376), so parallel workers run without the queue lock.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs:701-703 calls `mark_task_doing` for all runs; `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/context.rs:50-64` updates `.ralph/queue.json` without acquiring the queue lock, so concurrent workers can race on status visibility and queue writes.
plan: Define the concurrency contract: either (A) prevent multiple run loops via a dedicated run-loop dir lock, or (B) atomically reserve tasks by marking them `Doing` under the queue lock during parallel selection.
Implement the chosen approach so a task selected for parallel execution is not re-selectable by another run loop during the selection→spawn window.
Make queue writes safe in parallel-worker mode (either move status transitions into the orchestrator, or ensure `mark_task_doing` acquires the queue lock when not already held).
Add regression tests covering success and failure modes (e.g., lock contention between two run loops, and no duplicate task selection while a parallel loop is active).
Verify with `make test` (or at minimum `cargo test -p ralph parallel` and the new contention test) and ensure the fix doesn't block normal queue/TUI operations unintentionally.
notes: Earlier scan evidence assumed parallel workers never marked tasks as `Doing`; current code does mark `Doing`, but only inside the spawned worker process, leaving a selection→spawn race window.
`mark_task_doing` writes `.ralph/queue.json` without taking the queue lock, which can cause concurrent write races when multiple parallel workers start at once.
Implemented queue lock for full parallel run loop to prevent duplicate task selection
Added select_next_task_locked() requiring held lock at compile time
Added QueueLockMode::AcquireAllowUpstream for parallel workers
Added unit tests for select_next_task_locked
Added integration test for parallel supervisor contention

tasks.title: Avoid copying heavy gitignored directories into each workspace
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/sync.rs:165-207 iterates every path returned by `git::ignored_paths` and copies it into each workspace; directories are copied recursively via `sync_path_recursive` with no allow/deny/size filtering.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/sync.rs:209-229 recursively traverses ignored directories and copies every entry, so ignored build outputs can balloon sync time and disk usage.
.gitignore:8 ignores `target/`; .gitignore:12 ignores `.ralph/cache/parallel/`; .gitignore:15 ignores `.ralph/cache/plans`, all of which can become large and are eligible for copying when present.

crates/ralph/src/git/status.rs:80-117 lists ignored paths via `git ls-files -i -o --exclude-standard -z --directory`, so ignored directories (e.g. `target/`) can appear (with trailing `/`) and get synced.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/sync.rs:294-325 test demonstrates current behavior copies ignored `.env` and an ignored directory into the workspace; any fix should preserve necessary ignored files (like `.env`) while skipping heavy directories.
plan: Decide and document a default policy for gitignored syncing: allowlist a small set of ignored files to copy (e.g. `.env`, `.env.*`) and denylist heavy ignored directories (`target/`, `node_modules/`, `.ralph/cache/`, `.venv/`, etc.).
Update `sync_gitignored` to enforce the allow/deny policy before any directory traversal while keeping the existing workspace self-skip behavior intact.
Update/extend tests in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/sync.rs` to assert `.env` still syncs, but ignored `target/` (and/or `.ralph/cache/parallel/`) does not.
Add focused unit tests for path filtering edge cases (trailing `/`, nested paths, file vs dir).
Verify with `cargo test -p ralph sync_ralph_state` (and run `make ci` before marking the task done).
notes: This is currently amplified by `.gitignore` including multiple `.ralph/cache/*` entries (e.g. parallel state and plans), which are likely to be large and should not be replicated into every workspace.
There are already unit tests proving ignored paths are copied today; adjust them to encode the intended allow/deny behavior rather than deleting coverage.
Implemented should_sync_gitignored_entry() filter to prevent copying heavy gitignored directories to workspaces
Allowlist: only .env files sync; Denylist: target/, node_modules/, .ralph/cache/, etc. are skipped
Removed sync_path_recursive() and directory traversal logic
Added 8 unit tests for the filter function
Updated integration test to verify allow/deny behavior
make ci passes (1681 tests)

tasks.title: Add queue-aware guidance to merge conflict prompt
evidence: crates/ralph/assets/prompts/merge_conflicts.md:1-13 is generic conflict-resolution guidance and does not mention `.ralph/queue.json` / `.ralph/done.json` semantics (ordering matters) or queue validation steps.
crates/ralph/src/promptflow.rs:228-235 `build_merge_conflict_prompt` forwards directly to `prompts::render_merge_conflict_prompt` without inspecting conflict file names for queue/done special-casing.
crates/ralph/src/prompts_internal/merge_conflicts.rs:18-38 only expands variables and replaces `{{CONFLICT_FILES}}`; it does not add any queue-aware instructions.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:247-263 builds and runs the merge-conflict prompt, then validates queue/done JSON before committing; the prompt itself still lacks guidance about preserving queue ordering/IDs while resolving conflicts.
plan: Update `crates/ralph/assets/prompts/merge_conflicts.md` to include an explicit section for when `.ralph/queue.json` or `.ralph/done.json` are conflicted (preserve task ordering, do not drop/rename IDs, keep valid JSON).
Add/extend tests in `crates/ralph/src/prompts_internal/tests/merge_conflicts.rs` to assert the rendered prompt includes the new queue-aware guidance (and still replaces `{{CONFLICT_FILES}}`).
Verify with `cargo test -p ralph merge_conflicts` and then run the full gate via `make ci`.
notes: Merge conflict prompt rendering currently lives in `crates/ralph/src/prompts_internal/merge_conflicts.rs`, with prompt assembly in `crates/ralph/src/promptflow.rs` and execution/queue validation in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs`.
Added queue-aware guidance to merge conflict prompt
Updated merge_conflicts.md with special section for .ralph/queue.json/.ralph/done.json
Added 3 tests verifying queue guidance is included in rendered prompts
All 5 merge_conflict tests pass
Full make ci passes (1684+ tests)

tasks.title: Respect max_tasks when resuming with in-flight parallel state
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:220 initializes `tasks_started` to 0 even when `state_file.tasks_in_flight` may be non-empty after `load_state` + `prune_stale_tasks_in_flight`.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:249-253 gates spawning on `tasks_started < opts.max_tasks` and `guard.in_flight().len() < settings.workers`; neither condition accounts for `state_file.tasks_in_flight` records when resuming.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs:71-82 excludes `state_file.tasks_in_flight` IDs from selection, preventing duplicates but not enforcing `max_tasks`/worker capacity for resumed in-flight records.
plan: After loading+pruning state, treat `state_file.tasks_in_flight.len()` as already-started work: initialize `tasks_started` from it and include it in the spawn-capacity check.
Add unit tests for resume scenarios (state file with N tasks_in_flight) asserting no new workers start when `max_tasks` or worker capacity would be exceeded.
Run `cargo test -p ralph parallel`, then `make ci`.
notes: Current orchestration tracks capacity via `guard.in_flight()` only; resume state is represented in `state_file.tasks_in_flight`, so both max-tasks and worker-cap accounting should consider that vector (post-prune).
Fixed RQ-0568: Respect max_tasks when resuming with in-flight parallel state
Added helper functions: effective_in_flight_count(), initial_tasks_started(), can_start_more_tasks()
Updated tasks_started initialization to count resumed in-flight tasks
Added periodic pruning of stale records in main loop
Added 3 unit tests covering resume scenarios
All 55 parallel tests pass, make ci passes

tasks.title: Reconcile PR status to avoid permanently excluded tasks
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs:83-86 excludes any task with a PR record where `merged` is false.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:327 and 507 are the only call sites for `mark_pr_merged`, so manual merges/closures never update `state.json`.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/state.rs:78-82 only flips `merged` when invoked; parallel state is persisted at `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json`.
crates/ralph/src/git/pr.rs:43-55 only fetches `mergeStateStatus` and `isDraft` from `gh pr view`; it does not capture PR lifecycle (`OPEN`/`CLOSED`/`MERGED`).
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:165-206 checks mergeability via `git::pr_merge_status`, but nothing reconciles `ParallelPrRecord.merged` from current GitHub PR status.
plan: Extend `crates/ralph/src/git/pr.rs` to fetch PR lifecycle status (e.g., `state` and/or `isMerged`) via `gh pr view --json ...` and expose a small typed helper.

On parallel loop startup in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs`, refresh each `state_file.prs` record where `merged` is false; if the PR is not `OPEN`, remove it (or otherwise mark it resolved) and persist `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json`.
Update `collect_excluded_ids` in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs` so tasks are excluded only for PRs that are still `OPEN` (not just `merged == false`).
Add tests with a stubbed `gh` binary to simulate `OPEN`/`CLOSED`/`MERGED` PRs; assert the state file is reconciled and task selection no longer excludes closed/merged PRs.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph parallel` (and any new `git::pr` tests) and confirming reconciliation behavior.
notes: Parallel PR records currently only model a boolean `merged`, but the failure mode includes PRs that are closed without merge (or merged outside Ralph) and therefore should be reconciled from GitHub before exclusion decisions.
Extended git::pr to expose PR lifecycle (state, is_merged) via gh pr view
Added ParallelPrLifecycle enum to state.rs with backward compatibility via serde(default)
Added reconcile_pr_records() helper that queries GitHub and updates state file on startup
Updated parallel/mod.rs to reconcile PRs after pruning stale tasks, filter existing_prs and completed_workspaces to only open/unmerged PRs
Updated worker.rs collect_excluded_ids() to only exclude tasks with OPEN PRs (not closed/merged)
Added comprehensive tests including stubbed gh binary tests for reconciliation

tasks.title: Fix parallel state.json to auto-correct and auto-populate git branch
evidence: User request: Fix parallel state to auto-correct/auto-populate the git base branch so switching branches doesn't require manually deleting `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json`.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs loads `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json` and hard-fails when `existing.base_branch != current_branch` (error: "Parallel state base branch '{}' does not match current branch '{}'"), preventing any automatic recovery.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/state.rs persists `base_branch` as a required field in `ParallelStateFile` and uses it as the fallback base for PR info when persisted PR records are missing fields.

crates/ralph/src/git/branch.rs rejects detached HEAD ("Parallel run requires a named branch"), so base branch auto-population must use the named branch string returned by `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`.
plan: Reproduce the failure by running a parallel loop on one branch, switching branches, and re-running parallel to hit the base_branch mismatch error.

Design a safe recovery policy for base branch mismatch: only auto-correct when the persisted state has no active in-flight tasks and no open/unmerged PR records; otherwise keep failing fast with an actionable message.

Implement recovery in `run_loop_parallel`: on mismatch, load state, prune/reconcile, then either (a) update `state_file.base_branch` to the current branch and persist, or (b) bail with guidance that includes the state file path and why it's unsafe to auto-correct.
Ensure new state files are created with the current branch and that recovery preserves merge settings overrides (merge_method/merge_when) when appropriate.

Add unit/integration tests covering: (1) mismatch + no in-flight work => base_branch auto-updated, (2) mismatch + in-flight tasks/open PRs => still errors, (3) state file missing => created with current branch.
Update docs (`docs/cli.md`, `docs/workflow.md`) to document base-branch mismatch behavior and the new auto-correction rules.
Run `make ci`.
notes: The current failure happens before stale task/PR reconciliation, so moving or augmenting the mismatch handling may be necessary to determine whether auto-correction is safe.
tasks.request: Fix the state.json file to auto-correct and auto-populate and set the git branch properly. User previously ran a parallel run on branch '02022026' but has since changed to 'main' and this fails unless user manually clears .ralph/cache/parallel/state.json.
